name: Password Reset Flow Rate Limit
objective: Limit repeated password reset and account recovery requests to reduce account takeover pressure, OTP flooding, and recovery-flow abuse.
risk_mitigated: Password reset abuse, mailbox flooding, recovery endpoint enumeration, and account takeover attempts using reset workflows.
potential_side_effects: Shared IP ranges, helpdesk-assisted onboarding, and training environments may legitimately generate bursts of recovery traffic.
tuning_notes: Start with a threshold of 3 to 5 reset attempts per IP over 10 minutes. Add bypasses for trusted support networks only when operationally necessary.
deployment_notes: Apply to POST requests for /forgot-password, /reset-password, /password/reset, and equivalent recovery paths. Start in log mode for 48 hours if your application serves large shared customer networks.
monitoring_notes: Watch for repeated reset attempts against the same paths, low bot scores, and bursts from anonymizing providers or unusual countries. Coordinate with identity telemetry to spot downstream mailbox or MFA abuse.

cloudflare_rate_limit_config.description: Password reset and recovery abuse rate limit
